stack: Q5_K_M GGUF (--no-mtp, no MTP/speculative-decoding head, quantized from the f16 master), llama-server -ngl 999 --parallel 4 --jinja, CTX 20480
llamacpp_build: version: 1 (8f114a9) built with GNU 13.3.0 for Linux aarch64

measured.n_dataset_note: NOT like-for-like with f16/Q8/Q6 (all n=121). This is the wp_judge-filtered 121-row subset MINUS exactly the one parse-failure row (index 109). Confirmed by direct count: 121 total indexed rows, 1 parse failure, 120 scored -- not a second, unrelated drop. Because rho is computed on a different (n-1) example set than the other three rungs, its delta below is not a perfectly clean like-for-like comparison, though the practical effect of dropping 1/121 rows on Spearman rho is small.
measured.parse_fail_root_cause: Index 109's generation entered a degenerate repetition loop and never emitted the closing structured-rubric JSON before hitting the shared max_tokens=2048 cap. Response is 8021 characters (~2048 tokens at this model's byte-per-token ratio, consistent with running to the token budget). Tail of the response repeats the fragment 'Issue: format_error() is a simple string construction with no IOPerformance: severity low (8/10) —' verbatim multiple times back-to-back, then truncates mid-fragment with no closing brace/JSON. This is a per-sample generation-quality failure at Q5_K_M's reduced precision on this specific prompt, not a harness bug: same parser (eval.output_parsers.parse_judge_scores), same max_tokens (2048), same dataset/index mapping as every other rung, all of which had parse_fail=0 at n=121.

gate.pass_caveat: This 'pass' is on the rho-vs-floor criterion only, computed on the n=120 like-for-like-with-a-caveat subset above. It does NOT certify Q5 as defect-free -- see reliability block below, which is the criterion that actually disqualifies this tier from automatic shipment.

reliability.note: The ONLY tier in this ladder with a nonzero parse failure. f16 (n=121), Q8 (n=121), Q6 (n=121) all scored parse_fail=0. For a judge whose entire output contract is a parseable structured rubric, a parse failure is a hard functional failure, not a soft quality wobble -- and this project's own v3 PKG-03 protocol treats zero parse failures as the bar (see output/packaging/pkg03_quantization_ladder.json Q8 entry, 'parse_fail_all_arms: 0'). 1/121 is weak statistical evidence on its own (wide binomial CI at this n) and could be a single unlucky sample -- it is NOT claimed as proof Q5_K_M is systematically less reliable. But it is the only tier-discriminating signal this ladder actually measured (see noise_floor_finding: rho cannot discriminate), and a hard failure mode is one where conservatism is cheap.

deltanet_state_precision.note: PKG4-02 below-Q8 clause. Q5's DeltaNet ssm_* tensors match the Q5_K_M tier's nominal type exactly (120 stay F32, 90 track Q5_K -- identical split to Q8 and Q6). No state tensor dropped below the requested tier.
